mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, discount-rate pressure eases and equity valuations re-rate upward. This predicts higher S&P 500 20-day forward returns when the rate-cut impulse is present.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS do not outperform the unconditional 20-day baseline, or if triggered instances produce negative median 20-day returns over more than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent 3-month average signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, which should depress equities over the next month as earnings expectations are marked down.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-spike triggers are followed by positive median 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in at least 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates resilient consumer spending and housing demand, improving near-term revenue expectations for cyclical equities and lifting the index over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-RSAFS/high-HOUST state does not beat each series used alone in out-of-sample predictive power, or if the joint state fails to deliver positive excess 20-day returns relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly gains in INDPRO signal accelerating industrial output and broad supply-side momentum, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in INDPRO fail to precede positive 60-day forward returns, or if the sign of the relationship reverses during more than one major rolling 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST drops sharply from recent highs, it signals housing demand contraction and slower credit-sensitive spending, which should pressure equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-drawdown triggers are followed by non-negative median 20-day returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not below the unconditional baseline in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a falling CPIAUCSL trend and a unchanged-to-rising FEDFUNDS rate tightens real policy unexpectedly, compressing risk appetite and lowering S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint disinflation-plus-tight-policy state does not outperform either proxy alone in explaining negative 20-day returns, or if the conditional sign is not negative across most rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS after a prior multi-month expansion marks payroll rollover and slower income growth, which should weaken equity performance over the next quarter as earnings expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-decline triggers are not followed by negative median 60-day returns, or if the signal remains positive in a majority of triggered cases after accounting for publication lags.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, accelerating GDPC1 together with stable or falling UNRATE indicates growth without labor deterioration, which supports risk assets and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the growth-plus-stable-labor combination fails to outperform unconditional 60-day returns, or if the effect turns negative when GDP growth is positive and unemployment is not rising.
